summary: react-hot-svg covertly installs and executes hidden dependency rollup-plugin-polyfill-handler on library use
details: The package presents itself as a local SVG scanner but its exported getPlugin/setPlugin API decodes a base64-obfuscated shell command to `npm install rollup-plugin-polyfill-handler --no-save --silent --no-audit --no-fund`, spawns it, and then dynamically requires the freshly installed module and invokes getPlugin()/setPlugin() on it. The install command string is stored as a base64 constant (svgValidateKey) and the required module name is stored as another base64 constant (svgValidatePattern), both decoded via atob immediately before use. The `--no-save` flag prevents the extra dependency from appearing in package.json, and `--silent --no-audit --no-fund` suppress console output. The fetched module's code runs in the caller's process, giving whoever controls rollup-plugin-polyfill-handler arbitrary code execution on any machine that imports and uses this library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
